{{Quote|This place looks like it was hit harder than we hit Spindrift, Commander, what happened?|Wald upon arriving at Chinshassa|<ref name="MC60" />}}
'''Wald''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] stationed at the Salvation base on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]], shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. He flew a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] and served under the command of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="MC60" />

==Biography==
Wald was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] with long, wavy brown hair. He wore [[spectacles]] and had a full mustache and beard. He wore the standard-issue X-wing flight suit and carried a [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]].<ref name="MC60" />

[[File:Wald_blaster.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Wald fighting the Em'liy]]
Wald was part of an excursion that followed fellow pilot [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] to the planet [[Shalyvane]]. Shira told the other members of her squad that Shalyvane was her home world and that a tribe of alien nomads known as the [[Em'liy/Legends|Em'liy]] had been conducting raids on the village of [[Chinshassa]]. The pilots disembarked from their fighters and engaged the Em'liy at the [[Circle of Kavaan/Legends|Circle of Kavaan]]. With the help of Skywalker's [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], the pilots succeeded in driving the opposing forces away.<ref name="MC60" />

It wasn't until later that Wald and the others learned that their fellow pilot, Shira Brie, was in fact an Imperial spy, conspiring with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to calculate the coordinates to the new Rebel base at [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]].<ref name="MC63">[[Star Wars (1977) 63|''Star Wars'' (1977) 63]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Wald was created by [[Marvel Comics]] writers, [[David Michelinie]] and [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]]<ref name="MC60" /> and bears a marked resemblance to Simonson.

Although other Rebel pilots from issues of the Marvel run set between [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] have been [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] into being members of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]],<ref name="NEGTC Shira">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{C|under "Shira Brie"}}</ref> Wald has never been identified as a Rogue.
